nodes.context: Powerful New York street gang led by Paul Kelly, with mostly Italian members but also some Irish and Jewish.
Rival gang to the Five Points Gang, led by Monk Eastman, predominantly Jewish members.
Small gang formed by Nathan Kaplan after leaving the Five Points Gang, involved in labor slugging.
Small gang formed by Johnny Spanish after splitting from Kaplan, rival to Kid Dropper Gang.
Labor slugging gang led by Jacob 'Little Augie' Orgen, rival to Kaplan's operations.

edges.context: Nathan Kaplan was a member of the Five Points Gang before forming his own gang.
The two gangs were initially allied before a bitter rivalry developed.
A bitter personal rivalry and violent war erupted between Kaplan and Spanish over a woman.
Kaplan and Orgen fought over control of labor slugging, leading to violent shootouts.
The Five Points Gang and Eastman Gang were rival street gangs in New York.
